preview_text:
The model favors Mexico heavily at 71%, leaving Czech Republic with just 7% to win. This gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in the forecast, positioning Mexico as a clear favorite on the statistical evidence.

The win-draw-loss spread widens markedly when examined as a whole. Mexico's 71% win probability substantially exceeds the combined 29% chance of either a draw or Czech Republic victory. A draw remains plausible at 23%, but the modal outcome is a Mexico win by a clear margin. The match is unlikely to feel competitive in a statistical sense.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. Books and Polymarket both place Mexico much lower—around 42–44%—while substantially elevating Czech Republic's chances to near 30%. This 28-percentage-point gap on the Mexico line is large enough to reflect genuine disagreement about the teams' relative strength. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the discrepancy warrants scrutiny from bettors and analysts.
